RECDATA.FacilityDescription:
Overview

Rampart Range Campground sits inside the Pike National Forest at 9,200 feet, surrounded by ponderosa pine and rocky outcroppings.
Sites accommodate tents and small RVs up to 35 feet. Flush toilets and potable water are available on-site.

Activities

Hiking, mountain biking, and OHV riding on the Rampart Range Road corridor.
Fishing is permitted in nearby Schubarth Creek.
RECDATA.FacilityKeywords: camping, tent, RV, hiking, mountain biking, OHV, fishing, Pike National Forest, ponderosa pine, 9200 feet

RECDATA.FacilityDescription:
Overview

Stillwater Campground is located on the shore of Green Mountain Reservoir in Summit County at an elevation of 7,950 feet.
The campground offers stunning views of the Gore Range and direct access to the reservoir.

Activities

Boating, waterskiing, kayaking, and shore fishing for rainbow trout and kokanee salmon.
Nearby trails connect to the Eagles Nest Wilderness.
RECDATA.FacilityKeywords: camping, reservoir, boating, waterskiing, kayaking, fishing, trout, kokanee, wilderness, Gore Range, Summit County

RECDATA.FacilityDescription:
Overview

Camp beneath a rare triple waterfall in western Colorado. The campsite sits adjacent to Rifle Falls, a 70-foot cascading waterfall fed by East Rifle Creek.
Limestone caves and lush hanging gardens surround the area.

Activities

Cave exploration, birdwatching, photography, and swimming in the creek pools.
The park connects to Rifle Gap State Park with additional fishing and boating.
RECDATA.FacilityKeywords: camping, waterfall, caves, birdwatching, swimming, photography, limestone, western Colorado, state park

RECDATA.FacilityDescription:
Overview

South Rim Campground overlooks the dramatic gorge of Black Canyon of the Gunnison National Park.
At 8,300 feet, the campground provides electric and non-electric sites with pinyon-juniper woodland setting.

Activities

Rim hiking, inner canyon climbing, ranger-led programs, and world-class trout fishing in the Gunnison River below.
Dark-sky stargazing is exceptional here year-round.
RECDATA.FacilityKeywords: camping, national park, canyon, stargazing, climbing, trout fishing, dark sky, pinyon, juniper, Gunnison

RECDATA.FacilityDescription:
Overview

Elbert Creek Campground is the staging ground for climbers and hikers attempting Mount Elbert, Colorado's highest peak at 14,440 feet.
Located in San Isabel National Forest at 10,000 feet along Lake Creek Road.

Activities

Fourteener summit attempts on Mount Elbert and Mount Massive, creek fishing, and wildlife viewing.
Elk and mule deer are frequently spotted in the meadows.
RECDATA.FacilityKeywords: camping, fourteener, Mount Elbert, summit, hiking, fishing, elk, mule deer, San Isabel National Forest, 14440

RECDATA.FacilityDescription:
Overview

Mueller State Park offers over 130 campsites set among aspen groves and open meadows on the west slope of Pikes Peak.
The park spans 5,000 acres with panoramic views of the Sangre de Cristo and Collegiate Peaks.

Activities

More than 90 miles of trail for hiking, mountain biking, and horseback riding.
Wildlife is abundant — black bears, mountain lions, elk, and wild turkeys are resident species.
RECDATA.FacilityKeywords: camping, aspen, meadow, Pikes Peak, hiking, mountain biking, horseback, wildlife, bear, elk, turkey, state park
